stem: A IT operations data backup uses 3 parallel storage devices for redundancy. The data recoverable requires at least one storage device to function. storage device 1 has a reliability between 73% and 85% (uncertain due to limited operational data). storage device 2 has a reliability of 78%. storage device 3 has a reliability of 96%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all storage devices simultaneously, estimated between 3.0% and 5.9%. What is the overall probability that the data recoverable?

ground_truth.answer: P(data recoverable) ranges from 0.9387 to 0.9687. Point estimate: 0.9543.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 storage devices fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.27 × 0.22 × 0.04 = 0.0024. P(works) = 0.9976.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.15 × 0.22 × 0.04 = 0.0013. P(works) = 0.9987.
With common-cause failure (3.0%-5.9%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9387, 0.9687].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
